ACTIVITIES:

Eversource Energy, a leading utility company listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), operates primarily in the northeastern United States, delivering reliable electric power and natural gas services to millions of customers. The company's core activities encompass electric transmission and distribution, natural gas delivery, and water services, with a strong emphasis on infrastructure maintenance and energy efficiency initiatives. In recent years, Eversource has experienced rapid growth in its renewable energy segments, particularly offshore wind projects and clean energy solutions, driven by increasing demand for sustainable power sources and supportive regulatory environments. These expansions, including investments in large-scale wind farms and electric vehicle charging infrastructure, have positioned the company as a key player in the transition to greener energy, while enhancing its competitive edge and long-term revenue potential. Overall, Eversource's strategic focus on innovation and sustainability supports its commitment to operational excellence and community service.

May 11, 2026 - Dividend Announcement 1 of 2:

AVISTA QUARTERLY DIVIDENDS STEADY

Avista today announced a quarterly dividend of 49.25c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 49.25c. The ex-dividend date is Tuesday, May 19, 2026 and the record date is Tuesday, May 19, 2026 and it is payable on Friday, June 12. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended May 11 were $1.96. This reflects actual dividend yield of 4.8% at the last price of $40.82.

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

May 11, 2026 - Dividend Announcement 2 of 2:

ORMAT TECHNOLOGIES QUARTERLY DIVIDENDS STEADY

Ormat Technologies today announced a quarterly dividend of 12.0c per share for Q1/2026; unchanged from the previous quarter (Q4/2025) dividend of 12.0c. The ex-dividend date is Wednesday, May 20, 2026 and the record date is Wednesday, May 20, 2026 and it is payable on Wednesday, June 03. Total dividends per share paid in the 12 months ended May 11 were 48.0c. This reflects actual dividend yield of 0.4% at the last price of $121.67.

ACTIVITIES:

Ormat Technologies, Inc., a U.S.-listed company on the New York Stock Exchange, is a global leader in renewable energy solutions, specializing in the development, construction, ownership, and operation of geothermal power plants. The company's core activities encompass geothermal energy generation, recovered energy power systems that convert waste heat into electricity, and energy storage solutions, including battery systems that support grid stability and renewable integration. Ormat's fastest-growing segments include its energy storage business, driven by increasing demand for reliable, scalable solutions amid the global shift to sustainable energy, as well as expansions in recovered energy projects that leverage industrial waste heat for efficient power production. This strategic focus has positioned Ormat as a key player in the transition to cleaner energy sources, with a strong track record of operational excellence and innovative technology deployment across international markets.

value.Title: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJK-9YP3-SBH9-52T3-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ RYAN TO BOLSTER PROPERTY TAX CAPABILITIES WITH MERITAX ADVISORS BUY

Ryan Companies has announced the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, a Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm, with the move made official on the 10th of February 2026. The deal broadens Ryan's property tax capabilities for commercial real estate owners amid a more technology-driven landscape, emphasizing enhancements in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services.Meritax Advisors, founded in 1995, has earned a strong reputation for property tax representation across varied commercial real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value for institutional and private clients in Texas and beyond. The integration is designed to augment Ryan's existing property tax services by bringing deeper analytics, real-time intelligence and predictive insights to support technology-driven advocacy for clients.Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, 'With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we’re adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape.' The firm notes that the acquisition will strengthen Ryan's ability to serve commercial real estate owners not only in Texas but also on a national scale, leveraging Ryan's global footprint and proprietary technology.As part of the integration, key Meritax leaders will join Ryan, including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with 20 additional team members. The combined organization underscores Ryan’s commitment to expanding its Property Tax practice in a competitive market and aligns with the broader goal of serving more than 74,000 clients worldwide with a 7,100-strong professional team.

+ RYAN COMPANIES' ANNA GRIFFIN APPLIES AIR FORCE EXPERIENCE TO SOUTHWEST CONSTRUCTION PROJECTS

In a new segment of the AZ Big Podcast, Anna Griffin, who oversees construction operations for Ryan Companies US, discussed how leadership and resilience are informed by her military career. The interview was recorded 5 days ago and aligns with Veterans Day by featuring Griffin, who moved from military service to directing construction projects across the Southwest. Griffin had recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan, where she served as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing. She observed that "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," adding, "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, she directs project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education, and, after nearly four years with the firm, her leadership helps maneuver the intricacies of construction sites. She underscores integrity, accountability, and mission readiness as enduring traits from her military days that inform civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, explores notable stories in Arizona's business scene. Sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo, a prominent law firm, the show seeks to illustrate how effective leadership and collaboration propel results across industries. Griffin's appearance arrives at a moment when Arizona honors its veterans and recognizes the marketable skills they bring to the workforce. Listeners are invited to hear Griffin's compelling narrative and learn how veterans like her contribute meaningfully to fields such as construction. As she continues her role with Ryan Companies, her path illustrates the resilience and adaptability of veterans transitioning to new responsibilities, reinforcing that military-honed skills remain valuable across sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHY-DKN3-RTX9-G0WY-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - UPDATED INSIGHTS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHY-K3T3-RRNM-H31S-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 6.9% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 6.9% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced total revenue of $570m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 6.9% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 7.6% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JHH-MPX3-S7MH-60YF-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 6.9% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 6.9% sequential rise in Quarterly Revenue

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced total revenue of $570m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 6.9% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and down 7.6% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025].

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

Quarter-ended31 Mar 202631 Mar 2025EPS, c11198PCP growth in EPS %137.7Revenue, $ million570617PCP growth in Revenue %-7.61.3Net Profit, $ million92.079.0PCP growth in Net Profit %16.511.3

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

value.Title: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH3-HP93-S4TB-X1MT-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - INDUSTRY DEVELOPMENTS INCLUDING RYAN COMPANIES AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ RYAN TO BOLSTER PROPERTY TAX CAPABILITIES WITH MERITAX ADVISORS BUY

Ryan Companies has announced the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, a Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm, with the move made official on the 10th of February 2026. The deal broadens Ryan's property tax capabilities for commercial real estate owners amid a more technology-driven landscape, emphasizing enhancements in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services.Meritax Advisors, founded in 1995, has earned a strong reputation for property tax representation across varied commercial real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value for institutional and private clients in Texas and beyond. The integration is designed to augment Ryan's existing property tax services by bringing deeper analytics, real-time intelligence and predictive insights to support technology-driven advocacy for clients.Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, 'With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we’re adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape.' The firm notes that the acquisition will strengthen Ryan's ability to serve commercial real estate owners not only in Texas but also on a national scale, leveraging Ryan's global footprint and proprietary technology.As part of the integration, key Meritax leaders will join Ryan, including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with 20 additional team members. The combined organization underscores Ryan’s commitment to expanding its Property Tax practice in a competitive market and aligns with the broader goal of serving more than 74,000 clients worldwide with a 7,100-strong professional team.

+ RYAN COMPANIES' ANNA GRIFFIN APPLIES AIR FORCE EXPERIENCE TO SOUTHWEST CONSTRUCTION PROJECTS

In a new segment of the AZ Big Podcast, Anna Griffin, who oversees construction operations for Ryan Companies US, discussed how leadership and resilience are informed by her military career. The interview was recorded 5 days ago and aligns with Veterans Day by featuring Griffin, who moved from military service to directing construction projects across the Southwest. Griffin had recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan, where she served as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing. She observed that "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," adding, "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, she directs project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education, and, after nearly four years with the firm, her leadership helps maneuver the intricacies of construction sites. She underscores integrity, accountability, and mission readiness as enduring traits from her military days that inform civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, explores notable stories in Arizona's business scene. Sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo, a prominent law firm, the show seeks to illustrate how effective leadership and collaboration propel results across industries. Griffin's appearance arrives at a moment when Arizona honors its veterans and recognizes the marketable skills they bring to the workforce. Listeners are invited to hear Griffin's compelling narrative and learn how veterans like her contribute meaningfully to fields such as construction. As she continues her role with Ryan Companies, her path illustrates the resilience and adaptability of veterans transitioning to new responsibilities, reinforcing that military-honed skills remain valuable across sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JH9-GXP3-S940-Y344-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - RECENT NEWS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND INDUSTRY PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ RYAN TO BOLSTER PROPERTY TAX CAPABILITIES WITH MERITAX ADVISORS BUY

Ryan Companies has announced the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, a Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm, with the move made official on the 10th of February 2026. The deal broadens Ryan's property tax capabilities for commercial real estate owners amid a more technology-driven landscape, emphasizing enhancements in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services.Meritax Advisors, founded in 1995, has earned a strong reputation for property tax representation across varied commercial real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value for institutional and private clients in Texas and beyond. The integration is designed to augment Ryan's existing property tax services by bringing deeper analytics, real-time intelligence and predictive insights to support technology-driven advocacy for clients.Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, 'With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we’re adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape.' The firm notes that the acquisition will strengthen Ryan's ability to serve commercial real estate owners not only in Texas but also on a national scale, leveraging Ryan's global footprint and proprietary technology.As part of the integration, key Meritax leaders will join Ryan, including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with 20 additional team members. The combined organization underscores Ryan’s commitment to expanding its Property Tax practice in a competitive market and aligns with the broader goal of serving more than 74,000 clients worldwide with a 7,100-strong professional team.

You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, she directs project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education, and, after nearly four years with the firm, her leadership helps maneuver the intricacies of construction sites. She underscores integrity, accountability, and mission readiness as enduring traits from her military days that inform civilian projects. urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-31BS-00000-00CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisSTOCKCLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM RYAN COMPANIES AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY

PermID4296495921Websitehttp://www.claycorp.comIndustryConstructionAddress2199 Innerbelt Business Center Drive Overland, Missouri 63114

ACTIVITIES:

Clayco Construction Company, Inc., founded in 1984 by Bob Clark, is a design-build firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 3,800 employees and $7.6 billion in 2024 revenue, it serves commercial, industrial, and life sciences sectors. Clayco offers architecture, engineering, and construction services through subsidiaries like Lamar Johnson Collaborative and Concrete Strategies. Known for projects like the Novus Headquarters, it emphasizes sustainable, LEED-certified buildings. In 2024, Clayco expanded into renewable energy and data center construction. Its culture fosters innovation, safety, and diversity, with robust training programs. Competing with DPR Construction, Clayco stands out for its integrated approach and fast-track delivery. Recent initiatives include advancing technology-driven construction and eco-friendly practices.

+ CLAYCO CONSTRUCTION COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ CLAYCO UNVEILS POWER AND ENERGY UNIT AIMING FOR 1,000 JOBS BY 2027

Chicago-based contractor Clayco has formed a new division dedicated to large solar and storage projects across the country. Unveiled on 4th March 2026, the unit, Clayco Power and Energy, targets almost 1,000 U.S. jobs by 2027 and expects roughly $300 million in annual revenue.Clayco Power and Energy will provide design, engineering, construction, and procurement for solar and battery storage systems. It is currently developing a 350-acre solar-plus-storage project in Manteno, Illinois, about an hour south of Chicago, with construction due to start this June.The move aligns with a projected rise in electricity demand of 15%–20% by 2030, driven by data centers, manufacturing, and the shift to electrified transportation. Notably, last year saw solar plus storage accounting for the majority of new capacity, underscoring the market tailwinds behind solar deployment.

+ CLAYCO PARTNERS WITH DEEP ATOMIC ON U.S. NUCLEAR-POWERED AI DATA CENTER AT IDAHO NATIONAL LABORATORY

Clayco, a leading design-build firm specializing in complex mission-critical facilities, has announced its collaboration with Swiss-American energy company Deep Atomic to support submissions to the U.S. Department of Energy for a nuclear-powered AI data center campus planned at Idaho National Laboratory, a project that could usher in a new era of energy-efficient computing.On 12th February 2026, Clayco said it would provide early-stage delivery planning, constructability analysis, and integrated design-build expertise as part of the consortium. The firm’s track record in delivering large-scale data centers positions it to help ensure the campus can be designed and built safely, efficiently, and at scale. Bob Clark, Executive Chairman and Founder of Clayco, stressed that confidence in project delivery is essential for a successful DOE submission.Deep Atomic’s approach blends advanced nuclear energy with AI data center infrastructure to deliver reliable, low-carbon power for next-generation computing. The partnership aims to establish a scalable, sustainable platform that could position the United States as a leader in clean-energy AI infrastructure; Deep Atomic founder and CEO William G. J. Theron said the mission is to accelerate deployment of advanced nuclear power to meet performance and sustainability needs, with Clayco guiding execution planning for the DOE submission.

+ CLARK URGES UNIFIED ACTION TO REBUILD FOUNTAIN PARK AFTER MAY 16 TORNADO

ST. LOUIS, Mo. (First Alert 4) - Nearly a year after the May 16 tornado devastated North St. Louis, Clayco CEO Bob Clark told business and civic leaders that rallying with political figures is essential to craft a unified reconstruction strategy for areas already under stress. Standing in Fountain Park, he said the recovery has been slow but the neighborhood shows potential for renewal.Clark said Clayco moved quickly after the storm, coordinating with Mayor Cara Spencer and city services along with local partners. The company’s long-term rebuilding effort includes funding and staffing community programs with Albion and Washington University to improve quality of life and keep the city moving forward. He praised residents for supporting each other in the immediate aftermath in the absence of formal plans.Looking ahead, he described Fountain Park as a beacon of hope and noted developments that were already under way before the tornado, including city programs such as the St. Louis Development Corporation and the Land Clearance for Redevelopment Authority that promote new housing and help families find safe, affordable homes. He stressed that everything starts with the civic community and a shared will to navigate recovery.Clark framed recovery as a gradual process, drawing on parallels to Hurricane Katrina and underscoring the need to start somewhere. He reiterated a vision of incremental progress: one house at a time, one block at a time, one brick at a time.Despite challenges, including more than 65% of affected property owners not applying for city programs, the city extended the deadline for its private property assistance program to February 28 to boost participation. Clark pointed to visible improvements since the tornado and cited the Delmar Divine development as evidence that teamwork can drive meaningful change.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ RYAN COMPANIES [2 News That Matters]

+ RYAN TO BOLSTER PROPERTY TAX CAPABILITIES WITH MERITAX ADVISORS BUY

Ryan Companies has announced the acquisition of Meritax Advisors, a Texas-based commercial property tax consulting firm, with the move made official on the 10th of February 2026. The deal broadens Ryan's property tax capabilities for commercial real estate owners amid a more technology-driven landscape, emphasizing enhancements in valuation strategy, litigation management and large-portfolio advisory services.Meritax Advisors, founded in 1995, has earned a strong reputation for property tax representation across varied commercial real estate sectors and manages tens of billions of dollars in assessed value for institutional and private clients in Texas and beyond. The integration is designed to augment Ryan's existing property tax services by bringing deeper analytics, real-time intelligence and predictive insights to support technology-driven advocacy for clients.Damon Chronis, President of U.S. Operations at Ryan, stated, 'With Meritax Advisors joining Ryan, we’re adding a disciplined, client-focused team with deep knowledge of the Texas property tax landscape.' The firm notes that the acquisition will strengthen Ryan's ability to serve commercial real estate owners not only in Texas but also on a national scale, leveraging Ryan's global footprint and proprietary technology.As part of the integration, key Meritax leaders will join Ryan, including Ryan Chismark, Charlie Honea and Mark Kline, along with 20 additional team members. The combined organization underscores Ryan’s commitment to expanding its Property Tax practice in a competitive market and aligns with the broader goal of serving more than 74,000 clients worldwide with a 7,100-strong professional team.

+ RYAN COMPANIES' ANNA GRIFFIN APPLIES AIR FORCE EXPERIENCE TO SOUTHWEST CONSTRUCTION PROJECTS

In a new segment of the AZ Big Podcast, Anna Griffin, who oversees construction operations for Ryan Companies US, discussed how leadership and resilience are informed by her military career. The interview was recorded 5 days ago and aligns with Veterans Day by featuring Griffin, who moved from military service to directing construction projects across the Southwest. Griffin had recently returned from a seven-month deployment to Jordan, where she served as aircraft maintenance squadron commander for the 165th Airlift Wing. She observed that "Construction has so many parallels to my military service," adding, "You’re still serving — just in a different way. You’re building something lasting, something that supports people and communities." At Ryan Companies, she directs project management teams across healthcare, multifamily housing, and education, and, after nearly four years with the firm, her leadership helps maneuver the intricacies of construction sites. She underscores integrity, accountability, and mission readiness as enduring traits from her military days that inform civilian projects. The AZ Big Podcast, hosted by Michael Gossie and Amy Lindsey, explores notable stories in Arizona's business scene. Sponsored by Burch & Cracchiolo, a prominent law firm, the show seeks to illustrate how effective leadership and collaboration propel results across industries. Griffin's appearance arrives at a moment when Arizona honors its veterans and recognizes the marketable skills they bring to the workforce. Listeners are invited to hear Griffin's compelling narrative and learn how veterans like her contribute meaningfully to fields such as construction. As she continues her role with Ryan Companies, her path illustrates the resilience and adaptability of veterans transitioning to new responsibilities, reinforcing that military-honed skills remain valuable across sectors.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGK-4Y83-RSP9-70SW-00000-00Why this Oregon utility is betting big on California's energy market 2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:41:14ZLexisNexisBUSINESSWhy this Oregon utility is betting big on California's energy market Portland-based PacifiCorp is the first utility outside California to participate in the market, which pools generation and transmission assets to reduce costs and improve reliability.Ted Sickinger

PacifiCorp on Friday became the first utility outside California to join a new wholesale energy market, a move it says will generate substantial savings for customers, greater reliability and reduced greenhouse gas emissions.

The Portland-based utility, Oregon's second largest, joined the California Independent System Operator's extended day-ahead market, or EDAM, which went live Friday. CAISO is a non-profit that manages the high voltage grid for 80% of California and part of Nevada, and is now extending participation in its day-ahead market to utilities outside its borders.

In effect, PacifiCorp is pooling its generation and transmission assets with other western utilities and allowing CAISO to determine how to optimize the flow of power to meet each utility's forecast demand with the lowest cost power and transmission.

PacifiCorp is the first utility outside California to go live in the market, which currently includes that state's biggest investor-owned utilities: Southern California Edison, Pacific Gas & Electric and San Diego Gas & Electric.

PacifiCorp has a small number of customers in California but has not previously participated in the day-ahead market. It's a key player in its expansion, however, because it operates in six states and owns the largest privately held transmission system in the western United States. Other utilities are expected to follow suit, which would increase the market's efficiency, according to Mike Wilding, vice president of energy supply management at PacifiCorp.

"These organized markets are the best tool that we have available to control our costs for our customers," Wilding said in an interview. "The way they do that is they leverage larger footprints, a larger pool of generation and more transmission connectivity than PacifiCorp has on its own, which now means you can move electricity where it needs to go more effectively and more efficiently."

Portland General Electric has pledged to join the market in October, and other utilities in California and New Mexico are slated to join in 2027. Utilities in Idaho and Nevada are also evaluating joining.

Regionwide coordination of energy markets has been on the table for decades but has been a bugaboo for policy makers and utilities in the Northwest. Unlike the grid in the East and Midwest, the transmission system in the West is highly fragmented into individual utility fiefdoms.

But the need for better coordination and resource sharing has become more acute due to surging industrial energy demand, forecast supply shortfalls that could undermine reliability, and rising prices.

PacifiCorp's modeling shows EDAM could eventually save its customers $300 million annually. In addition, Wilding said the market will free up transmission capacity and boost system reliability by enabling regions with excess power to assist other areas experiencing extreme weather. And it will support greenhouse gas reductions by managing variations in output of renewable energy sources, like wind and solar, across a wider area.

The day-ahead market builds on the success of an energy imbalance market that CAISO launched in 2014. PacificCorp was also the first to join that market, which gives utilities access to energy to manage short term supply and demand imbalances. It spans 11 western states and has generated over $8.6 billion in benefits for participants since it launched.

One big player that won't be joining EDAM is the Bonneville Power Administration, which sells power from 31 hydroelectric dams and a nuclear plant, and which operates three quarters of the high voltage grid in the Northwest. Under pressure from customers leery of giving California too much control over the region's power grid, BPA last year made the controversial decision to join Markets+, a separate wholesale market operated by the Southwest Power Pool and headquartered in Arkansas.

At the time, BPA said it preferred the governance structure and reliability program of Markets+, despite studies showing more substantial savings from joining EDAM.

The federal agency's decision is being challenged in the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals by a coalition of environmental and consumer groups. They contend the decision will drive up energy bills in the region, undermine reliability and decrease access to renewables. The groups say the decision violates BPA's obligations under the Northwest Power Act to ensure cost effective and reliable power for the Northwest and protect fish and wildlife in the Columbia River Basin.

The California Independent System Operator has made significant changes to its own governance structure to accommodate expansion of the day-ahead market, transitioning to a new decision-making authority and laying the plans for a new regional organization.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

IDACORP, Inc., a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary Idaho Power Company, focusing on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. The company's core business centers on delivering reliable electric services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Idaho and Oregon, emphasizing cost-effective energy solutions and infrastructure maintenance. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, including wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to rising demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory policies. This growth is further bolstered by investments in grid modernization and new customer connections, such as those supporting data centers and population increases, enabling IDACORP to enhance its market position in a competitive energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 16th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.

Press Release article 2 of 5, Source: Company Website, 275 words

February 19: IDACORP, Inc. Announces Fourth Quarter and Year-End 2025 Results, Initiates 2026 Earnings Guidance

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA) reported fourth quarter 2025 net income attributable to IDACORP of $43.6 million, or $0.78 per diluted share, compared with $37.9 million, or $0.70 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2024. For the full year ended December 31, 2025, IDACORP reported net income attributable to IDACORP of $323.5 million, or $5.90 per diluted share, compared with $289.2 million, or $5.50 per diluted share, in 2024.

"IDACORP's earnings in 2025 were at the top-end of the previously increased earnings guidance range. Results benefited from customer growth, rate changes, lower income tax expense, and the use of tax credits under the company's Idaho regulatory mechanism," said IDACORP President and Chief Executive Officer Lisa Grow. "Partially offsetting those benefits were higher depreciation and financing costs from infrastructure investments."

"We accomplished a great deal in 2025, from breaking ground on our Boardman-to-Hemingway transmission line to achieving record system reliability for our customers. As we move ahead, we are heavily focused on affordability and reliability for our customers as we continue to execute on our infrastructure development plans, with 2026 being another year of substantial capital investment for our company," Grow added.

IDACORP is initiating its full-year 2026 earnings guidance in the range of $6.25 to $6.45 per diluted share with the expectation that Idaho Power will use less than $30 million of additional tax credits available under the Idaho regulatory mechanism in 2026. The earnings guidance assumes normal weather conditions and power supply expenses throughout the year.

February 05: Events Calendar: IDACORP Schedules Fourth Quarter 2025 Earnings Release & Conference Call

BOISE, Idaho--(BUSINESS WIRE)-- IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE:IDA) will report its fourth quarter results on Thursday, February 19, in a news release before the stock markets open. The company will hold an analyst conference call that day at 2:30 p.m. Mountain Time (4:30 p.m. Eastern Time) to discuss the fourth quarter 2025 earnings.

All parties interested in listening may do so through a live Webcast or by calling 855‑761‑5600 for listen-only mode. The passcode for the call is 9290150. The conference call logistics are posted on the company's website (www.idacorpinc.com) and will be included in the company's earnings news release. Slides will be included during the conference call. To access the slide deck, please visit www.idacorpinc.com/investor-relations. A replay of the conference call will be available on the company's website for a period of 12 months and will be available shortly after the call.

Background Information

IDACORP, Inc. (NYSE: IDA), Boise, Idaho-based and formed in 1998, is a holding company comprised of Idaho Power, a regulated electric utility; IDACORP Financial, an investor in affordable housing and other real estate tax credit investments; and Ida-West Energy, an operator of small hydroelectric generation projects that satisfy the requirements of the Public Utility Regulatory Policies Act of 1978. Idaho Power, headquartered in vibrant and fast-growing Boise, Idaho, has been a locally operated energy company since 1916. Today, it serves a 24,000-square-mile service area in Idaho and Oregon. With 17 low-cost hydropower projects at the core of its diverse energy mix, Idaho Power's residential, business, and agricultural customers pay among the nation's lowest prices for electricity. Its 2,100 employees proudly serve more than 660,000 customers with a culture of safety first, integrity always, and respect for all.

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

In this MD&A section of this report, the general financial condition and results of operations for IDACORP and its subsidiaries and Idaho Power and its subsidiary are discussed. The discussion of IDACORP's and Idaho Power's general financial condition and results of operations for 2024 compared with 2023 can be found in their Annual Report on Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. See Part II - Item 7 - MD&A in that report for further information on the companies' prior period results of operations. Also refer to "Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements" and Part I - Item 1A - "Risk Factors" in this report for important information regarding forward-looking statements made in this MD&A section and elsewhere in this report.

INTRODUCTION

IDACORP is a holding company whose principal operating subsidiary is Idaho Power. IDACORP's common stock is listed and trades on the New York Stock Exchange under the trading symbol "IDA". Idaho Power is an electric utility whose rates and other matters are regulated by the IPUC, OPUC, and FERC. Idaho Power generates revenues and cash flows primarily from the sale and distribution of electricity to customers in its Idaho and Oregon service areas, as well as from the wholesale sale and transmission of electricity. On February 13, 2026, Idaho Power entered into a definitive agreement to sell its Oregon electric distribution business and associated distribution assets, as well as certain Oregon transmission assets, to OTEC. The closing of the transaction is subject to various conditions, including approvals of the OPUC, IPUC, and FERC.

urn:contentItem:6JHV-2T23-S4NP-P04S-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced net profit of $92.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 30% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 27.6% sequentially from 87.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.11 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-YV13-SHFW-V4WN-00000-00Quarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 20262026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:49:10ZLexisNexisQuarterly Report Analysis Quarter ending 31 March 2026: Avista reports 30% sequential rise in Quarterly Net Profit Corporate Wire Date: 05 May 2026 23:33 EDT Release Date: May 05, 2026

AMERICAN RESULTS

Avista (NYSE:AVA), announced net profit of $92.0m for the quarter-ended 31 March 2026 [Q1/2026], up 30% from the previous quarter [Q4/2025] and up 16% from the year-earlier period [Q1/2025]. Earnings per share (EPS) were up 27.6% sequentially from 87.0c in Q4/2025 to $1.11 in Q1/2026.

Major changes compared with previous corresponding period [Mar 31, 2026 vs Mar 31, 2025]:

Favourable Changes:

- Net profit was up 16.5% from $79.0m to $92.0m

- EPS growth 13.3% from $0.98 to $1.11

- EBIT to Sales was up from 21.1% to 25.4%

- Profit before tax to Sales was up from 14.7% to 18.4%

- Profit after tax to Sales was up from 12.8% to 16.1%

Unfavourable Changes:

- Total revenue was down 7.6% from $617.0m to $570.0m

- Interest expenses to Sales was up from 6.3% to 7%

Note:

- Tax expenses to Sales was up from 1.9% to 2.3%

Quarterly Report (Quarter ending 31 Mar 2026)

Compared with the Previous Corresponding Period [PCP; Q1/2025], year-over-year [y.o.y.] EPS was up 13.3%, Revenue was down 7.6% and Net Profit was up 16.5%.

ACTIVITIES:

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs.

MARKET POSITION:

Avista Corporation (NYSE:AVA), the NYSE's 21st largest Utility company by market cap, increased $1.01 (or 2.5%) in the trailing 12 months to May 05, 2026 to close at $40.93. Compared with the S&P 500 Index which has risen 27.6% over the same period, this is a relative price change of -25.1%. Average daily volume of 661,092 shares in the trailing year was 1.2 times average daily volume in the previous year.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

Avista Corporation, a utility holding company headquartered in Spokane, Washington, and listed on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: AVA), focuses on providing reliable energy services in the Pacific Northwest. Its core business activities encompass the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity and natural gas, primarily through its subsidiary Avista Utilities, which serves approximately 400,000 electric and 360,000 natural gas customers across Washington, Idaho, and parts of Oregon and Montana. The company operates a diverse energy portfolio that includes hydroelectric, thermal, and wind-powered facilities, emphasizing efficient resource management and infrastructure maintenance. In recent years, Avista's fastest-growing segments have been its renewable energy initiatives, such as wind and solar projects, fueled by rising demand for sustainable options and supportive regulatory policies. This strategic expansion enhances Avista's competitive position in the evolving energy sector while supporting environmental goals and customer needs. It is the NYSE's 24th largest Utility company by market capitalisation.

SECTION 3 PAST YEAR PRESS RELEASES

3.1 Press Releases and Corporate Wire

Press Release article 1 of 5, Source: Avista Corporation, 300 words

May 13: Regions first microgrid begins operations at Spokane community center

SPOKANE, Wash., May 12, 2026 -- Avista Utilities today announced the start of operations for the regionsfirst community-based microgrid. This marks the launch of an innovative energy system designed to help the Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Center (MLK Center) stay open and continue serving the community during extended, unplanned power outages.

The Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. Family Outreach Center is a non-profit, community- based social service center located in East Central Spokane, one of the most ethnically diverse neighborhoods in Spokane County.

Located at the community center, the microgrid brings together solar power, battery storage and natural gas to provide added reliability when the power goes out, such as during extreme weather or other emergencies.

The systems solar and battery storage will also help the MLK Center reduce its standard energy bill, allowing for more resources to be redirected into critical community programs.

Were here for families every day, and during emergencies, people rely on us even more. This energy system helps us continue that work and keep our doors open for the East Central neighborhood, which means so much to me, said Freda Gandy, MLK Center executive Director.

On a typical day, the MLK Center will continue using power from the grid, supplementing energy from onsite solar and battery storage. During extended outages, batteries can help keep power flowing, with natural gas backup available if an outage lasts longer than a few hours. This helps ensure the Center remains open and operational.

The project was made possible through grant funding from Avistas Named Communities Investment Fund (NCIF) and grant support from the Washington State Department of Commerce.

Avistas NCIF program is the only one of its kind at a Washington utility.

May 11: Avista quarterly dividends steady

Press Release article 4 of 5, Source: West Corporation, 113 words

May 06: Avista Corp. Board Declares Common Stock Dividend

SPOKANE, Wash., May 06, 2026 -- Avista Corp.s (NYSE: AVA) board of directors has declared a quarterly dividend of $0.4925 per share on the companys common stock, yielding an annualized dividend of $1.97. The common stock dividend is payable June 12, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on May 19, 2026.

The declaration of dividends is at the sole discretion of the board of directors. The board considers the level of dividends on a regular basis, taking into account numerous factors, including financial results, business strategies, and economic and competitive conditions.

Press Release article 5 of 5, Source: West Corporation, 330 words

May 05: Avista Corp. Reports Q1 2026 Financial Results, Confirms 2026 Utility Earnings Guidance

SPOKANE, Wash., May 05, 2026 -- Avista Corp. (NYSE: AVA) today reported net income based on GAAP of $92 million, or $1.11 per diluted share, for the first quarter of 2026, compared to $79 million, or $0.98 per diluted share, in 2025. Non-GAAP utility earnings1 were $91 million, or $1.10 per diluted share, compared to $82 million, or $1.01 per diluted share in 2025. Avista Corp. is confirming its 2026 non-GAAP utility earnings guidance2 with a range of $2.52 to $2.72 per diluted share.

CEO Perspective

Strong performance in the first quarter demonstrates our focus on fundamentals: safety, reliability, and sound operational and financial execution. Our continued investments ensure we meet the needs of the communities we serve and also build long-term value for our customers, communities and shareholders. We are on track to meet our 2026 earnings guidance and are confident in the opportunities ahead, said Heather Rosentrater, President and CEO of Avista.

Analysis of First Quarter 2026 GAAP Earnings

Net income for the first quarter of 2026 increased compared to the first quarter of 2025 primarily due to increased utility margin resulting from the effects of our general rate cases and net investment gains at our other businesses compared to net investment losses in the first quarter of 2025.

Analysis of 2026 Non-GAAP Utility Earnings

The following table presents the changes in non-GAAP utility earnings and non-GAAP utility earnings per diluted share for the first quarter of 2026, as compared to the first quarter of 2025. It also outlines the various after-tax factors that contributed to these changes (dollars in millions, except per-share data):

(a)The tax impact of each line item was calculated using Avista Corp.'s federal statutory tax rate of 21 percent.

(b)Electric utility margin decreased as a result of the removal of revenues related to the recovery of Colstrip costs, partially offset by other effects of our general rates cases.

SECTION 2 CORPORATE PROFILE

2.1 Activities

IDACORP, Inc., a holding company listed on the New York Stock Exchange, operates primarily through its subsidiary Idaho Power Company, focusing on the generation, transmission, distribution, and sale of electricity in the Pacific Northwest region of the United States. The company's core business centers on delivering reliable electric services to residential, commercial, and industrial customers in Idaho and Oregon, emphasizing cost-effective energy solutions and infrastructure maintenance. Among its fastest-growing segments are renewable energy initiatives, including wind and solar projects, which have expanded rapidly due to rising demand for sustainable power and supportive regulatory policies. This growth is further bolstered by investments in grid modernization and new customer connections, such as those supporting data centers and population increases, enabling IDACORP to enhance its market position in a competitive energy landscape. It is the NYSE's 16th largest Conventional electricity company by market capitalisation.
